insight: The hydration breaks in the 2026 FIFA World Cup have had a notable impact on the United States team's performance, with the team gaining momentum in both of the two breaks they've had, resulting in a mean increase of 0.150 points per minute. This suggests that the breaks have given the team a chance to regroup and come back stronger, which is particularly evident when they weren't pressing before the break, as they managed to increase their momentum by an average of 0.300 points per minute. In contrast, when the United States was pressing before the break, their momentum remained relatively stable, with an average change of 0.000 points per minute, indicating that they were able to maintain their intensity even after the pause. Overall, the hydration breaks seem to be providing the United States team with a valuable opportunity to recharge and refocus, leading to improved performance on the field
